risks: Drag-and-drop card ordering requires a robust fractional indexing or gap-based sort order strategy — naive integer ordering causes re-index storms on every reorder
Real-time sync via WebSockets requires a conflict resolution strategy when two users move the same card simultaneously — last-write-wins is simple but lossy; OT/CRDT is complex
At scale, broadcasting every board event to all subscribers can overwhelm the WebSocket server — requires a pub/sub layer (e.g., Redis) to fan out events per board room
Activity log and notifications can grow unbounded — requires archival/pagination strategy early or query performance degrades significantly
features: Kanban board with customizable columns (e.g., To Do, In Progress, Done)
Drag-and-drop card reordering and column movement
Task cards with title, description, assignee, due date, labels, and priority
Real-time updates across collaborators on the same board
Team workspace with role-based access (Owner, Admin, Member, Viewer)
Comment threads on task cards with @mentions
File attachments on cards (images, PDFs, docs)
Activity log per card and per board
Due date reminders and notifications (in-app + email)
Board filtering by assignee, label, priority, or due date
Card archiving instead of hard delete
Invite members via email with role assignment
Board templates (e.g., Sprint, Bug Tracker, Content Calendar)
userFlows: User signs up → verifies email → creates Workspace → invites teammates via email → teammates accept invite and join Workspace
Member creates Board → adds Columns → creates Card → assigns to teammate → sets due date and priority → teammate receives notification
Member drags Card from 'In Progress' to 'Done' → all collaborators on board see update in real-time → ActivityLog records move event
Member @mentions teammate in Comment → mentioned user receives in-app notification → clicks notification → lands on specific card with comment highlighted
Member tries to delete a Column that contains active Cards → warned that N cards will be lost → must choose to archive cards or reassign them to another column before deletion proceeds
Invited user receives email but workspace is already at seat limit → invite link shows 'Workspace full' error → prompts Owner to upgrade or remove a member
Member applies filter (assignee = self, priority = High) → board renders only matching cards → filter state persists on page refresh via URL params
constraints: Real-time board sync latency under 300ms p95 for collaborators in same region
API responses under 200ms p95 for read endpoints excluding file uploads
Board must render and be interactive with up to 500 cards across columns without scroll jank
File attachments capped at 10MB per file
Email invitations must be delivered within 60 seconds of being sent
Core action (create card, move card) must be completable in under 3 clicks from board view
description: A project management web app centered on kanban-style task boards with real-time team collaboration. Teams can visualize work in progress, assign tasks, and coordinate across projects — targeting small-to-medium teams who find tools like Jira too heavy but need more than a basic to-do list.
mvpFeatures: Kanban board with fixed columns and drag-and-drop card movement
Task cards with title, description, assignee, due date, and priority
Team workspace with Owner and Member roles and email invite
openQuestions: Is the collaboration model workspace-scoped (all members see all projects) or project-scoped (members are invited per project)? This fundamentally changes the permission model and data access layer.
Should boards support a swimlane dimension (e.g., rows by epic or assignee) in addition to columns, or is a flat column layout sufficient? Swimlanes require a 2D card positioning model.
Is offline support or optimistic UI required? Optimistic updates with rollback change the frontend state management architecture significantly versus a simple request-then-update model.
